paragraphs.text:
## CuВІвҒәвҖ“Malonate Conditional Stability Constant at pH 4

### Data from SRD-46

**Malonic acid pKa values** (25 В°C, I = 0.1 M):

| Step | Equilibrium | pKa |
|------|-------------|-----|
| pKaвӮҒ | HвӮӮL вҮҢ HвҒә + HLвҒ» | **2.65** |
| pKaвӮӮ | HLвҒ» вҮҢ HвҒә + LВІвҒ» | **5.27** |

**CuВІвҒә + LВІвҒ» вҮҢ CuL** (ОІвӮҒ, 25 В°C, I = 0.1 M):

| vlm_id | log K | T (В°C) | I (M) |
|--------|-------|--------|-------|
| vlm_152316 | **5.04** | 25 | 0.1 |

I'll use **log ОІвӮҒ = 5.04** (25 В°C, I = 0.1 M) matched to the same conditions as the pKa values.

grounded_claims.evidence_snippet: By definition, pH = -log10[H+], so [H+] = 10^{-pH}. At pH 4, [H+] = 1Г—10^{-4} M. This relationship is not listed in the tool results.

paragraphs.text:
### Step 2 вҖ” Compute the ligand side-reaction coefficient Оұ_L

The fraction of total ligand present as the fully deprotonated form LВІвҒ» (the species that binds CuВІвҒә) is:

$$\alpha_L = \frac{K_{a1} \cdot K_{a2}}{[\text{H}^+]^2 + K_{a1}[\text{H}^+] + K_{a1} \cdot K_{a2}}$$

Substituting:

| Term | Value |
|------|-------|
| [HвҒә]ВІ | 10вҒ»вҒё |
| KaвӮҒВ·[HвҒә] | 2.24Г—10вҒ»Ві Г— 10вҒ»вҒҙ = 2.24Г—10вҒ»вҒ· |
| KaвӮҒВ·KaвӮӮ | 2.24Г—10вҒ»Ві Г— 5.37Г—10вҒ»вҒ¶ = 1.20Г—10вҒ»вҒё |
| **Denominator** | 10вҒ»вҒё + 2.24Г—10вҒ»вҒ· + 1.20Г—10вҒ»вҒё = **2.44Г—10вҒ»вҒ·** |
| **Numerator** | 1.20Г—10вҒ»вҒё |

$$\alpha_L = \frac{1.20 \times 10^{-8}}{2.44 \times 10^{-7}} = 0.0492 \approx 4.9\%$$

$$\log \alpha_L = \log(0.0492) = -1.31$$

paragraphs.text:
### Step 3 вҖ” Conditional Stability Constant

The conditional constant KвҖІ accounts for the fact that at pH 4, only a fraction of the ligand is in the reactive LВІвҒ» form:

$$K' = \beta_1 \cdot \alpha_L$$

$$\log K' = \log \beta_1 + \log \alpha_L = 5.04 + (-1.31) = \boxed{3.73}$$

| vlm_151949 | ligand_8873 | Propanedioic acid (Malonic acid) | H2L | O=C(O)CC(=O)O | 2.65 | 25 | 0.1 | HLвҶ’H2L | M_tot_2 | M_tot_26 |

| pKaвӮӮ (HLвҒ» вҶ’ LВІвҒ») | 5.27 |
| log ОІвӮҒ (CuВІвҒә + LВІвҒ» вҶ’ CuL) | 5.04 |
| Оұ_L at pH 4 (fraction as LВІвҒ») | 0.049 (4.9%) |
| log Оұ_L | вҲ’1.31 |
| **log KвҖІ (conditional, pH 4)** | **вүҲ 3.73** |

grounded_claims.evidence_snippet: vlm_152316 | beta_def_812 | logK 5.04 | 25 В°C | I 0.1 | equation [M] + [L] <=> [ML]
Derived at pH 4 from pKa 2.65 (HLвҶ’H2L) and 5.27 (LвҶ’HL); database lists pKa values but not Оұ_L.
log Оұ_L computed from pKa 2.65 and 5.27 at 25 В°C (DB provides pKa values; it does not list log Оұ directly).
logK 5.04 for [M]+[L]<=>[ML] plus log Оұ_L (from pKa 2.65, 5.27) вҮ’ log KвҖІ вүҲ 3.73; DB lists components, not KвҖІ.

paragraphs.text:
### Chemistry Insight

At pH 4, malonate sits between its two pKa values (2.65 and 5.27), so the **monoprotonated HLвҒ» form dominates** (~95% of free ligand). Only ~5% is the fully deprotonated LВІвҒ» that coordinates CuВІвҒә in the classical ML complex. This two-log-unit drop from the intrinsic log K of 5.04 to the conditional log KвҖІ вүҲ 3.73 illustrates why **pH control is critical** in complexation chemistry вҖ” raising pH from 4 to 7 would push Оұ_L вҶ’ ~1 and restore the full binding strength. Note also that SRD-46 reports a **CuHL species** (beta_def_779), which would contribute additional binding at pH 4 and make the true conditional constant somewhat larger than 3.73 if included.

*Source: NIST SRD-46 вҖ” vlm_151918, vlm_151949 (pKa); vlm_152316 (log K)*
